title: Magnitude 6.2 earthquake in Aomori causes injuries and damage in northern Japan
summary: A level 6 earthquake on the Japanese seismic intensity scale shook Aomori Prefecture, leaving at least 11 injured and raising concerns about possible aftershocks.

title: Japan presents imperial law reform to allow adoption of male heirs
summary: The government's plan, backed by the Speaker of the House of Representatives, aims to ensure a sufficient number of imperial family members through adoption.

title: Typhoon No. 7 threatens southwestern Japan with torrential rain and possible bullet train disruptions
summary: Evacuation orders are issued for 770,000 people, and warnings are issued over possible Shinkansen service cancellations on June 27.

title: Japanese national soccer team prepares for crucial match against Sweden in the World Cup
summary: Japan seeks to secure its place in the round of 16 in the match against Sweden, with anticipation over possible lineup changes and fan support.

title: Japan lowers travel advisory level for seven Middle Eastern countries after US-Iran agreement
summary: The decision is based on improved regional security following the nuclear understanding between Washington and Tehran.

title: LDP tax chief Yuko Obuchi resigns over opposition to consumption tax cut
summary: The former minister submitted her resignation to the ruling party's internal tax policy committee after disagreements with the leadership.

title: Japanese government delays target of reaching 1,500 yen minimum wage
summary: Prime Minister Takaichi had set the goal for the early 2030s, but the deadline is now being postponed.

title: Leak of names of judicial review committee members in Yamaguchi
summary: The Yamaguchi Public Prosecutors Office admitted to mishandling documents that led to the leak of personal data of the reviewers.

title: School fire in Tokyo caused by teacher drying clothes in classroom
summary: The fire started when a teacher left clothes drying near a heat source, forcing students to evacuate via emergency slides.

title: Japan considers unauthorized use of voice actor voices by AI illegal
summary: A Ministry of Justice panel is studying classifying the generation of fake images and audio with artificial intelligence as a violation of personality rights.

title: Takaichi government presents budget reform plan with no investment limit
summary: The new 'investment framework' allows applications with no upper limit, reflecting the prime minister's style and sparking debate on fiscal discipline.

title: Two earthquakes of magnitude over 7 shake Venezuela, causing over 160 deaths
summary: The earthquakes, followed by numerous aftershocks, led the government to declare a state of national emergency.

title: Hong Kong police arrest bookstore owners for selling books inciting hatred against the government
summary: The arrests occur under the national security law, as the crackdown on freedom of expression continues.

title: Emperor and Empress of Japan bid farewell to King and Queen of Belgium before returning to Tokyo
summary: During the state visit, Empress Masako described the meeting as 'very special'.

title: Nintendo to invest 121 billion yen in new technology center by 2029
summary: The company plans to build a new research and development base in Kyoto to strengthen its innovation capacity.

title: Honda and Nissan to standardize key components for future vehicles
summary: The two Japanese automakers agreed to share core parts for the next generation of vehicles, even after the breakdown of merger talks.

title: IOC to award $10,000 to each Olympic athlete for the first time
summary: The International Olympic Committee establishes a direct aid fund for all participants in the Olympic Games.

title: Nikkei 225 index reaches new all-time high, rising 3,191 points
summary: The Tokyo stock market saw a significant rise, closing at 72,366 yen and surpassing its previous high.

title: Trump insults Republican congressman at private dinner
summary: The former US president lashed out at the Republican representative, telling him 'sit down' and 'you lost the election' during an altercation at the Capitol.

title: Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo leaves over 1,000 infected and 277 dead
summary: The World Health Organization reports 1,094 confirmed cases, while a French doctor who returned after providing aid also became infected.
